key_insight: This review argues that adding solid lubricants to thermal spray coatings is a promising route to reduce friction and wear in demanding tribological applications.
engineering_relevance: The paper is relevant for designing longer-lasting, lower-maintenance surface coatings for aerospace, energy, and industrial machinery components.
method: Critical review of solid-lubricant-doped thermal spray coating literature
Comparison of coating systems, lubricant additives, and performance trends
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal review article rather than a new experimental dataset, so the evidence is based on synthesized literature in Journal of Thermal Spray Technology.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend strongly on spray process, lubricant stability, and coating adhesion, so results may not transfer directly across all operating conditions.
paper_title: Solid Lubricant-Doped Thermal Spray Coatings: A Critical Review
